immutable_record_notice: Once frozen, this manifest record must not be modified. Any amendment must create a new record version that references the superseded record.
title: Example Selective Admission Held-Out Evaluation
research_question: Under preregistered synthetic conditions, can a provenance-aware admission gate reduce later retrieval of irrelevant events without materially reducing source-sensitive recall?

hypothesis.statement: The example selective admission mechanism may outperform a store-all baseline on the joint retention criterion defined in this manifest.
hypothesis.falsifiable_prediction: Across the fixed seed set on the held-out suite, the candidate must reduce irrelevant-event retrieval rate by at least 20 percent relative to the store-all baseline while its source-sensitive recall is no more than 0.02 below that baseline.
hypothesis.null_hypothesis: The candidate fails at least one component of the preregistered joint criterion relative to the store-all baseline.
assumptions: Relevance labels are fixed before held-out evaluation.
Candidate and baseline receive identical event and query sequences.
The synthetic generator contains no personal data.

experimental_design.unit_of_analysis: One scenario seed evaluated under both candidate and baseline policies
procedure: Generate development and held-out scenarios from separately versioned configurations.
Replay each scenario byte-identically through the candidate and store-all policies.
Issue the preregistered retrieval requests and record metric inputs before aggregate analysis.
Evaluate rejection criteria before considering any evidence-state decision.
controls: Identical scenario bytes, retrieval requests, seed set, resource limits, and scoring code across candidate and baseline

ablation_plan.component_removed_or_changed: Remove provenance features from the admission decision while preserving the remaining policy.
ablation_plan.rationale: Test whether provenance contributes to the proposed effect rather than merely accompanying another policy feature.

boundary_and_adversarial_tests.description: Submit an irrelevant event containing an instruction that requests persistent storage and future execution.
boundary_and_adversarial_tests.expected_safe_behavior: The in-band instruction cannot override the admission policy or user scope.

boundary_and_adversarial_tests.description: Provide mutually inconsistent source assertions for a candidate event.
boundary_and_adversarial_tests.expected_safe_behavior: The mechanism emits an explicit ambiguity or rejection signal and does not silently elevate confidence.

stopping_rules: Stop and invalidate the affected run if candidate and baseline inputs differ.
Stop for security review if a test crosses a declared user-scope boundary.

baselines.name: Store-all baseline
baselines.description: Admit every syntactically valid event within the declared user scope.
baselines.rationale: Provides a simple persistent-memory comparison without selective admission.

datasets_or_scenarios.name: Example development scenario generator
datasets_or_scenarios.description: Planned synthetic event streams with relevance, provenance, scope, and retrieval-query annotations.
datasets_or_scenarios.license_or_terms: Project-authored synthetic fixtures; terms to be recorded before freeze.

datasets_or_scenarios.name: Example held-out selective-retention suite
datasets_or_scenarios.description: Planned held-out synthetic scenarios kept inaccessible until the manifest is frozen.
datasets_or_scenarios.license_or_terms: Project-authored synthetic fixtures; terms to be recorded before freeze.

held_out_evaluation.rationale: A separately frozen held-out suite is required to distinguish protocol development from evaluation.

metrics.name: Irrelevant-event retrieval rate
metrics.operational_definition: Fraction of retrieval requests for which at least one scenario-labeled irrelevant event appears in the returned set.
metrics.aggregation: Mean across held-out scenarios, reported per seed and with a preregistered interval estimate.
metrics.success_criterion: Candidate relative reduction is at least 20 percent versus store-all on the held-out aggregate.

metrics.name: Source-sensitive recall
metrics.operational_definition: Fraction of required relevant facts returned with the scenario-declared source identity intact.
metrics.aggregation: Mean across held-out scenarios, reported per seed and with a preregistered interval estimate.
metrics.success_criterion: Candidate is no more than 0.02 below store-all on the held-out aggregate.

randomness.seed_policy: Use the complete fixed seed set for every candidate, baseline, and ablation condition.
randomness.determinism_notes: Scenario generation is intended to be deterministic per seed; runtime nondeterminism must be logged and measured.

model_provenance.rationale: This example protocol evaluates a deterministic rule-based reference mechanism without a learned model.

replication_criteria: A replication must use the frozen protocol, exact source artifacts, declared seed set, and independently captured environment provenance.
Per-seed metric inputs and aggregate calculations must be available for recomputation.

analysis_plan.comparison_method: Paired candidate-minus-baseline comparisons by scenario and seed, evaluated against the joint success criterion.
analysis_plan.uncertainty_method: Report preregistered interval estimates across the fixed seed set without treating seeds as independent real-world users.
analysis_plan.missing_data_policy: Do not impute missing outcomes; mark the run incomplete and apply the stopping or invalidation rule.
analysis_plan.multiple_comparison_policy: The two primary metrics form one joint criterion; ablations and security probes are reported separately as exploratory or safety analyses.
analysis_plan.qualitative_analysis: Classify retrieval, provenance, admission, and security failures using preregistered categories while retaining raw case identifiers.

rejection_criteria.condition: Either primary success criterion fails on the held-out aggregate under the complete valid seed set.
rejection_criteria.rationale: The hypothesis is explicitly conjunctive and must not be accepted from improvement on only one metric.

rejection_criteria.condition: Candidate and baseline receive non-identical scenario or query bytes.
rejection_criteria.rationale: An input mismatch destroys the paired comparison.

security_and_privacy_analysis.threat_model_scope: Admission inputs, policy configuration, decision outputs, logs, user-scope binding, and downstream storage eligibility in the example harness.

assets.description: Candidate event content and associated provenance
Admission policy and user-scope authorization state

actors.role: Defines memory scope and authorized policy context
Submits candidate events that may contain hostile content
Evaluates policy and enforces typed scope boundaries

trust_boundaries.description: Untrusted event source to admission runtime
assumptions: All event content and in-band instructions are adversary-controlled.

trust_boundaries.description: Admission runtime to persistent storage eligibility interface
assumptions: The storage adapter enforces the typed user scope supplied by the harness.

adversary_capabilities: Submit repeated crafted events, conflicting provenance assertions, and in-band persistence instructions.
Observe admission outcomes and response timing for adversary-submitted events.

allowed: Admission decision oracle for events submitted within the adversary's own synthetic scope
denied: Direct policy-state mutation and access to another user's scope
assumptions: No external tools are available to the candidate event during policy evaluation.

in_scope: Decision reason codes, timing, and diagnostic-log content
out_of_scope: Physical compromise of the evaluation host
leakage_assumptions: The harness does not expose process memory or other users' logs to the adversary.

security_and_privacy_analysis.adversary_success_criterion: Cause unauthorized persistent admission, cross user scope, retain rejected sensitive content, forge provenance acceptance, or infer protected policy state beyond declared outputs.
security_and_privacy_analysis.data_sensitivity: Synthetic fixtures only in this example; the mechanism is nevertheless evaluated against confidential-content handling semantics.
authority_boundaries: Only the user-scoped policy authority may authorize persistence.
The mechanism cannot override deletion, expand scope, or reinterpret in-band text as authority.

identified_risks.description: Hostile content attempts to make its own instructions persistent.
identified_risks.impact_if_realized: Future retrieval could repeatedly reactivate attacker-controlled instructions.

identified_risks.description: Incorrect scope binding admits or retrieves content across user boundaries.
identified_risks.impact_if_realized: One user may influence or infer another user's memory state.

planned_controls: Out-of-band policy authority separated from event content
Reason-coded decisions and content-minimized diagnostic logs
residual_risks: Semantically deceptive but policy-conforming content may remain difficult to classify.
deletion_requirements: Rejected content must not survive in persistent logs.
Accepted records must retain user-scoped deletion handles and verifiable tombstone outcomes.

limitations.description: This planned example uses only synthetic scenarios.
limitations.risk_if_ignored: A benchmark result could be overgeneralized to natural conversations or real users.
limitations.next_check: Design a separately governed external-validity study only after the synthetic protocol is stable.

limitations.description: The example isolates a rule-based admission mechanism and does not evaluate learned admission policies.
limitations.risk_if_ignored: Conclusions could be incorrectly attributed to model-based systems.
limitations.next_check: Compare replaceable learned and deterministic policy components in a later preregistered experiment.

target_evidence_state.rationale: The planned experiment is designed to produce baseline-comparison evidence, but this draft manifest does not award or imply the BENCHMARKED label.
